dialogue.speaker: Dr. Cox
dialogue.text: People aren't chocolates. D'you know what they are mostly? Bastards. Bastard-coated bastards with bastard filling.

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Cox
dialogue.text: The simple fact that you actually seem to give a crap is the reason I took an interest in you to begin with. It's why I trust you as a doctor. Hell, it's why I trust you as a person.

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Kelso
dialogue.text: Ah, nothing in this world that's worth having comes easy.

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Cox
dialogue.text: Every one of our parents does considerable emotional damage, and from what I've heard, it just might be the best part of being a parent.

dialogue.speaker: Turk
dialogue.text: It sounds like you're asking me out on a man date.

dialogue.speaker: J.D.
dialogue.text: Turk, why are you so afraid of loving me?

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Cox
dialogue.text: Ladies and gentlemen, allow me to present: Man Not Caring.

dialogue.speaker: J.D.
dialogue.text: You're a very strange man, aren't you?

dialogue.speaker: J.D.
dialogue.text: I was a preemie.

dialogue.speaker: The Todd
dialogue.text: Appletini! Appletini, please!

dialogue.speaker: Elliot Reid
dialogue.text: Frick, frick, frickedy frick!

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Cox
dialogue.text: I have absolutely no idea what you're talking about, and I'm sure I never will, so why don't you go ahead and do whatever the hell it is you do, Newbie.

dialogue.speaker: J.D.
dialogue.text: It's funny how, no matter what, you always end up needing the people you pushed away.

dialogue.speaker: The Janitor
dialogue.text: I've been here seven years. You've been here, what, a day? You do the math.

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Cox
dialogue.text: Newbie, my drunk baby.

dialogue.speaker: Turk
dialogue.text: Helping or hurting, J.D.? Helping or hurting?

dialogue.speaker: J.D.
dialogue.text: No matter how much you care, some people just don't want to be saved.

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Cox
dialogue.text: There comes a time in every man's life when he's gotta stop thinking about himself and start thinking about somebody else.

dialogue.speaker: Carla Espinosa
dialogue.text: You can't keep blaming yourself. Just blame yourself once, then move on.

dialogue.speaker: J.D.
dialogue.text: Nothing in the world worth having comes without a little bit of fear.

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Cox
dialogue.text: I want you to be a doctor for the right reasons, Newbie. Not because somebody's making you do it, but because deep down, it's all you ever wanted to do.

dialogue.speaker: J.D.
dialogue.text: In the end, the questions that matter are pretty simple: Do you have any regrets? Are you happy?
